post_revision_checks: Open workbook with Excel/openpyxl and verify Sensitivity interpretation text exists.
Confirm Final Decision Table includes 'confidence/limitations' and cites sensitivity rows/cell refs.
Run validation_summary checks and verify 'formula_tie_outs' remains true.
extra_model_fields.verifier_recommendation: Fix grader output contradictions after resubmission and request human spot-check of workbook with openpyxl/Excel; ensure normalized actual_outputs include the new interpretation and confidence/limitations fields.

metadata_json.priority_notes: Changes are localized to Sensitivity, Decision, and Assumptions/Checks sheets; moderate risk of breaking links if hard-coded values are introduced - ensure formulas reference single-source cells before saving.

post_revision_checks: Open workbook with Excel/openpyxl and verify Sensitivity interpretation text exists.
Confirm Final Decision Table includes 'confidence/limitations' and cites sensitivity rows/cell refs.
Run validation_summary checks and verify 'formula_tie_outs' remains true.
extra_model_fields.verifier_recommendation: Fix grader output contradictions after resubmission and request human spot-check of workbook with openpyxl/Excel; ensure normalized actual_outputs include the new interpretation and confidence/limitations fields.

metadata_json.priority_notes: Changes are localized to Sensitivity, Decision, and Assumptions/Checks sheets; moderate risk of breaking links if hard-coded values are introduced - ensure formulas reference single-source cells before saving.

planned_changes: Add a 1-3 sentence interpretation block at the top of the Sensitivity sheet summarizing how discount-rate variations (0%, 5%, 10%) change each measure's net_cost_per_tCO2e, rank ordering, and recommendation confidence. Include explicit row/cell references for each sentence.

Modify the Final Decision Table to include a 'confidence/limitations' column. For each measure populate this column with a short statement referencing the Sensitivity sheet rows and any missing inputs (e.g., 'Sensitive to discount rate - see Sensitivity!A6:D6; discount rate assumed=5% (Assumptions!B2)')

On the Assumptions sheet, add/confirm base_discount_rate = 5% with an explanation and link the calculation cells to this single source (do not hard-code multiple copies). Update discount_rate_status to 'assumed' or 'resolved' as appropriate and record the rationale and reviewer note 'needs_review' if still applicable.

Ensure all repeated constants (discount rate, horizon_years) reference single-source input cells and add small validation/tie-out cells that call out the source ranges (e.g., 'Calc!B2 references Inputs!B3 - see Checks!C4').

Add a Checks or README sheet with a 'how to reproduce' short instruction (sheet map, key cells to edit, and how to re-run sensitivity) and list of key cell ranges used in the Final Decision Table for traceability.

Regenerate normalized actual_outputs (JSON) after the workbook change, ensuring the Sensitivity interpretation text and Final Decision Table 'confidence/limitations' content appear in normalized outputs. Include exact cell references in the validation_summary if feasible.

Resubmit updated workbook as attempts/revised/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] and request re-run of deterministic precheck and model grader. In the submission notes, ask a human reviewer to open the .xlsx with Excel/openpyxl to confirm cell formulas and textual interpretation (addresses grader runtime limitation).
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Close rubric gaps (ri_5 and partial ri_4): add explicit Sensitivity interpretation and per-recommendation confidence/limitations text; document discount-rate assumption and single-source inputs; include reproduction notes so graders can validate without opening the binary.
